projects._why_it_matters: This is the proof engine for the two PRIMARY brand.yml attributes (builds adopted tools / reproducible interactive research). A tool counts as a COSTLY signal only when adoption + reproducibility are evidenced — so every entry carries those fields, null until captured. Don't paste 100 names; import 100 with their proof.

issues.problem: PhD described 3 ways across sources (CS / Math+CS / computational-systems-biology major).
MBA in CV + name tag but absent from LinkedIn education; unverifiable from the two sources.
Overlapping company dates AND editorial rant ('The State Of Massachusetts drove this business out of business') embedded in two entries.
BA school (UC Santa Cruz) is single-sourced (CV); field word-order differs between sources.
Two emails in play (ni.brown@neu.edu vs bear@bearbrown.co) — which is the brand contact?
5 name variants incl. publication forms; pick one display name + one citation form.
Company-count metrics differ between CV and a LinkedIn post.
